violations.finding: Docstring contains environment-specific absolute path: 'Mirrors /root/knowledge-system/scripts/todo/ask.py structure.' — the /root/ prefix is machine-specific and will be wrong on any non-root environment.
violations.recommendation: Replace with non-environment-specific wording, e.g. 'Mirrors the ask.py todo-list structure.' or remove the line entirely.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified one missed violation: hardcoded absolute path /root/knowledge-system/scripts/todo/ask.py in spec.py docstring (Standard 2 no-hardcoded-values). One observation flagged about file-count metadata accuracy (no standards impact).

findings.issue: scripts/todo/spec.py:4 docstring contains environment-specific absolute path /root/knowledge-system/scripts/todo/ask.py — draft rationale incorrectly stated no hardcoded paths
findings.proposed_fix: Replace line 4 with non-environment-specific wording, e.g. 'Mirrors the ask.py todo-list structure.' or remove the line entirely.
findings.rationale: Standard 2 covers hardcoded file paths in script content. A docstring embedding an absolute /root/ path is environment-specific and violates the portability intent of the standard. Finding accepted and added as violation.

findings.issue: File-count metadata in audit description was slightly inaccurate (wc -l reports 11/11/11/58/41, not 12/12/12/59/42)
findings.rationale: No standards impact. The count difference is wc -l vs line count; does not affect violation findings.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Added no-hardcoded-values violation for spec.py:4 based on codex finding with PROPOSED_FIX. Observation-only finding logged but not actioned.
